selectWhen: The main target is SFX, music, cue timing, audio mix, or feedback clarity through sound.
The user needs help deciding whether audio should own the next review loop.
avoidWhen: The main issue is animation state logic or UI layout.
The main issue is gameplay rule ownership rather than audio presentation.

responsibilities: Frame audio feedback in terms of clarity, emphasis, and player response.
Handoff quickly when the real owner is animation, UI, or gameplay semantics.
mustNot: Claim runtime-backed MCP specialist resources in the first slice.
Pretend sound owns non-audio gameplay policy.
